description_long: This object is a lifelike representation of a medium-sized parrot with a predominantly olive-green body and a long, tapered tail. The bird's head is darker, almost grayish, with a pale beak that is slightly hooked, typical of many parrot species. Subtle yellow markings are visible around the neck and underparts, while the wings show hints of blue and green blending together. The tail is notably elongated and slender, extending well beyond the body and ending in a pointed tip. The overall feather pattern is smooth and uniform, with no distinct spots or bars, giving the bird a sleek appearance. The legs are not clearly visible, but the body posture and proportions suggest a typical perching bird adapted for urban environments.
description: A medium-sized, olive-green parrot with a long, pointed tail and a dark head, featuring a pale, slightly hooked beak and smooth, uniform plumage.
description_view_0: Side view showing the bird's elongated body, long tail, and the smooth transition of green and blue hues along the wings and back.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the narrow, pointed tail and the symmetrical alignment of the wings along the body.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring the first, with clear visibility of the beak, head, and the full length of the tail.
description_view_3: Frontal view focusing on the bird's head, beak, and the tapering shape of the body towards the tail.
attribute_plumage_pattern: solid olive-green with subtle yellow highlights and blue on the wings
